register.title: Create your account
register.accountEmployeeHint: Your manager gave you an invite code. You're joining an existing team — after signing up you'll enter the code to request to join.
register.accountOwnerHint: You own or run a restaurant. After signing up you'll create your own team and get the code to share with your staff.

errors.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts, try again in 15 minutes

stats.title: Statistics
stats.selectTeam: Select an active team in the navigation bar.
stats.managersOnlyHint: Team statistics are accessible to managers only.

dashboard.welcomeHint: You don't belong to any team yet. Ask your manager for the invite code to join yours.
dashboard.ownerHint: Are you a restaurant owner? Sign in again choosing « restaurant owner » at registration to create your own team.
dashboard.pendingHint: The manager must approve your request before you can access the schedule.
dashboard.pendingRefreshHint: This page will update automatically as soon as your request is approved.

teams.title: My teams
teams.emptyHint: You don't manage any team. If you're a restaurant owner, sign up again choosing « restaurant owner » at registration.

teamJoin.title: Join a team
teamJoin.codeHint: Ask a team manager for this code.

teamDetail.removeMemberMessage: {{first}} {{last}} will be removed from the team.
teamDetail.regenerateMessage: The old code will stop working.
teamDetail.resetPasswordMessage: A temporary password will be generated for {{first}} {{last}}. Share it with them immediately after.
teamDetail.leaveMessage: You will no longer see shifts for « {{name}} ».

tempPassword.title: Password reset
tempPassword.description: Temporary password for {{first}} {{last}}. Share it now — it will not be shown again after closing.
tempPassword.changeHint: Advise them to change it at next login (Profile page).

profile.title: My profile
profile.calendarHint: Subscribe your phone to a feed below. Your shifts will appear in Calendar (iPhone) or Google Calendar (Android) with native notifications 2 hours before each shift.
profile.iphoneTip: iPhone — quick method: Tap « Open in Calendar » above from Safari. The system will offer to add the calendar directly. One step.
profile.androidTip: Android: From a desktop, open calendar.google.com → Other calendars → + → From URL → paste the URL above. The calendar will then appear on your phone.
profile.subscribedTip: Once subscribed: your phone automatically checks for new shifts every hour. No action required. Native notifications 2h before.

settings.title: Team configuration
settings.managerOnly: Only a manager can configure the team.
settings.how1: Each member contributes a number of "coverage points" based on their level. Confirmed is the reference (100 points).
settings.how2: For each (service, station), the solver targets a points total = ideal. 100% = ideal reached; below = short-handed, above = comfortable backup.
settings.ratesHint: Gross hourly cost per level. The solver targets ideal coverage while minimising labour cost at equivalent skill. Initial values: HCR 2026 minimum rates.
settings.coefsHint: Member contribution to a service. Reference: Confirmed = 100.
settings.maxCouvertsHint: E.g. 100 covers/service on a full day. On quieter days, reduce the density in the Smart Planner — the target is divided accordingly.
settings.idealsTitle: Ideal staffing per service (= 100 % at full capacity)
settings.idealsHint: Coefficient sum the solver aims for at each (service, station). Dishwashing is merged into kitchen.

planning.title: Schedule
planning.selectTeam: Select an active team in the navigation bar.
planning.moveActive: Keyboard move: pick a "Drop the shift here" cell, or press Esc to cancel.
planning.deleteMessage: The {{type}} shift for {{name}} on {{date}} will be deleted.
planning.extraTitle: {{service}}: {{deficit}} coef missing to reach ideal — plan for an extra.
planning.extrasAlertTitle: {{n}} slot(s) need an extra this week

smartPlanner.title: Auto planner
smartPlanner.clearFirst: Start from an empty schedule (existing shifts of the week will be erased before applying)
smartPlanner.fullWeekTitle: This week is already well covered.
smartPlanner.fullWeekHint: Team members are close to their contractual target. Smart Planner only suggests a few additions — to rebuild the whole week from scratch, check « Start from an empty schedule » below.
smartPlanner.capacityHint: 50 % = quiet service (target halved). 100 % = full day. 150 % = heavy load.
smartPlanner.densityHint: Manager's estimate based on reservations and history.
smartPlanner.cloneMessage: All shifts from the previous week will be copied to this week. Duplicates will be skipped.
smartPlanner.clearMessage: All shifts this week will be permanently deleted.

memberEdit.title: Configure — {{name}}
memberEdit.setupBannerHint: Start with their contractual hours, then their role. The smart planner will only include them once both are set.
memberEdit.weeklyHoursHint: Used by the auto-planner. Set to 0 to exclude (executives, owner).
memberEdit.levelHint: Weights service coverage. Junior = half-power, Chef = lead.
memberEdit.polyvalenceHint: Primary station (★) is mandatory. Check additional stations this member can cover — the solver will assign them only if no specialist is eligible.

managerHelp.title: Manager guide
managerHelp.intro: How Crew schedules your team, and why. A concrete walkthrough of the concepts, business rules and alerts behind the app.

profiles.title: The 5 team-member levels
profiles.lead: Each team member has a level that weights their contribution to a shift. To cover a station at 100% (1.00), levels add up until they reach 1.00.
profiles.apprenti: first month on the station, needs constant supervision.
profiles.debutant: holds the station but needs help during rush.
profiles.autonome: autonomous on routine tasks, occasional coaching.
profiles.pilier: carries the station, can train a junior alongside.
profiles.referent: expert, can run the whole service (almost a chef).
profiles.example: Example — a Senior (0.60) + an Autonomous (0.40) cover the station at 100%. A lone Apprentice (0.15) only covers 15%: the solver will refuse to leave them alone if the « junior alone forbidden » rule is on.

polyvalence.title: Versatility (multi-station)
polyvalence.lead: Each member has a primary station (★ mandatory) and can check secondary stations they can cover when needed (kitchen, dish, floor, bar).
polyvalence.detail: The solver honours the primary specialty first. It moves a member outside their specialty only when no specialist is eligible (off, absent, capped on hours). This is the short-chain theorem in action: 2-3 versatility links per member absorbs the vast majority of contingencies.
polyvalence.source: Reference: Jordan & Graves, « Principles on the benefits of manufacturing process flexibility », Management Science 41(4), 1995.

smartPlanner.title: The Smart Planner
smartPlanner.lead: « Smart Planner » button on the Schedule page. The greedy solver produces a complete weekly draft in seconds — you then validate or tweak.
smartPlanner.step1: For every cell (day × shift), it identifies open stations and target level.
smartPlanner.step2: It filters eligible members (presence, versatility, weekly rest, HCR cap, junior-alone rule).
smartPlanner.step3: It picks those that maximize a combined score: coverage, target-hour smoothing, payroll savings, ideal-profile fit.
smartPlanner.step4: It moves to the next cell while updating counters (cumulated hours, remaining rest days, payroll).
smartPlanner.density: You set the expected density per day and shift (« lunch » / « dinner »). Three presets:
smartPlanner.calme: light service, fewer expected covers — the solver targets 50% of ideal staffing.
smartPlanner.charge: rush expected (full bookings, event) — 130% of ideal staffing.

hcr.title: HCR Convention constraints
hcr.lead: The solver and alerts rely on the French HCR Collective Convention (hotels, cafés, restaurants) and the Labor Code.
hcr.weekly: Maximum 48h per week per member (HCR art. 21 + L3121-20).
hcr.dailyKitchen: Maximum 11h per day for kitchen staff.
hcr.dailyOthers: Maximum 11h30 per day for the rest of the team (floor, bar, dish).
hcr.rest: At least 2 weekly rest days (consecutive or not).
hcr.juniorAlone: Custom business rule: a junior (Apprentice / Beginner) is never assigned alone on a station without at least one Senior or Lead present.
hcr.source: Sources: HCR Collective Convention (IDCC 1979); Labor Code L3121-20.

alerts.title: Alerts (banner above the schedule)
alerts.hcr: a member exceeds 48h/week or a daily cap, or is missing their 2 rest days. Blocking for validation.
alerts.fatigue: too many hours over a short window (KC & Terwiesch 2009, Management Science). Non-blocking but to monitor — quality and accident risk.
alerts.coverage: the staffing score for a shift drops below 0.80 (≈ 80% of target). The overall Service Health Score turns orange / red as soon as a shift dips too low.
alerts.source: Sources: KC & Terwiesch, « Impact of workload on service time and patient safety », Management Science 55(9), 2009; INRS ED 880 (mental and physical load).

algo.title: Solver algorithmic formulas
algo.lead: Smart Planner combines four mathematical building blocks. Understanding how they fit together helps you anticipate the solver's choices and tune your parameters (ideals, coefs, densities) for an even tighter schedule.
algo.coverageIntro: Each member contributes to a station's coverage based on their level (0.15 apprentice → 0.60 senior). Sum until the station's ideal is reached, scaled by the forecast density (Quiet 0.5 / Normal 1.0 / Busy 1.3).
algo.coverageDetail: Additive coefficients come from industrial-engineering literature (Bard 2003, Ernst 2004): the simplest way to model « 1 chef = 1 senior + 1 autonomous » without introducing non-linear terms that would make the solver NP-hard.
algo.scoringIntro: For each eligible candidate for a slot we compute a score. The solver always picks the highest. The weighting prioritizes hour deficit (a member far from their contractual target is favored), while respecting business preferences (station, default service, level).
algo.scoringDetail: The cost-aware penalty (λ = 0.5) means that, at equal deficit, the solver prefers the cheaper member — this is the line that enables the payroll-forecast optimization visible at the top of the schedule. Reference: Bard 2003, Operations Research, on cost-aware staffing formulations.
algo.iterationIntro: Instead of filling the schedule day by day (which would saturate Tue-Wed and leave Sat-Sun empty), the solver runs several passes over the full slot set, starting each pass with the most-uncovered ones. Each slot receives at most one member per pass. Continues until no progress is possible.
algo.iterationDetail: This « min-fill-first multi-pass » strategy guarantees that Sunday receives its first member before Tuesday gets its third. Complexity stays polynomial: O(passes × slots × candidates) with passes ≤ max levels per slot, typically < 100 iterations for a 24-slot week.
algo.healthDetail: Indicator shown at the top of the schedule. Each under-covered or overloaded slot drags the score down. Green > 75, orange 50-75, red < 50. A synthetic aggregate: a single number to decide whether the week is OK or needs re-planning.
algo.source: Theoretical sources: Bard 2003 « Selecting the appropriate input data set when configuring a permanent workforce »; Ernst et al. 2004 « Staff scheduling and rostering: a review of applications, methods and models »; Jordan & Graves 1995 (short chains in versatility); KC & Terwiesch 2009 (sustained workload). The concrete weights (10, 5, 3, 2, λ=0.5) were tuned through restaurant-profile experiments (200+ Playwright scenarios).

cost.title: Cost-aware optimization
cost.lead: Crew knows each member's hourly rate (entered in the member form or inherited from the HCR default scale).
cost.detail: When two assignments satisfy the constraints equally (level, versatility, HCR), the solver prefers the cheaper one. The weekly payroll forecast is shown at the top of the schedule — a reference to balance coverage against budget.

dnd.title: Drag-and-drop a shift
dnd.lead: On the Schedule page, each cell is draggable.
dnd.step2: Drag it to an empty cell (another day or another shift).
dnd.step3: Release: the shift moves immediately. If the move violates an HCR rule (e.g. exceeds 48h), an alert appears but the move is allowed (you remain the decision-maker).

ical.title: Calendar sync (iCal)
ical.lead: Each member can subscribe to their schedule from iPhone, Android or Outlook — no app to install.
ical.detail: Profile tab → « Calendar sync » card → scan the QR code or copy the .ics URL. Shifts appear in the native calendar with a notification 2h before each service.

workflow.title: Recommended weekly workflow
workflow.lead: The weekly ritual that saves the most time:
workflow.step1: Monday morning — review each member's form (contractual target hours, level, versatility).
workflow.step2: Set the expected density per day × shift (Quiet / Normal / Busy) based on bookings.
workflow.step3: Launch the Smart Planner — you get a complete draft in seconds.
workflow.step4: Adjust manually by drag-and-drop if needed (e.g. a member wants a specific Sunday off).
workflow.step5: Check no red HCR alert remains, then validate — members receive their shifts in their calendar.
